rationale: Only markdown/spec-doc and skill-graph watchers were found; code_graph paths self-heal on read, not by daemon.
Cleanup intervals exist but target session tables; memory bulk deletion also ignores delete_after.
Checked-in .github hook wiring makes the live path real, while shared docs still conflict with Copilot-specific guidance.
Current user runtime has hooks.json and codex_hooks enabled; repo settings alone remains an example, not live registration.

summary: Timer-driven cleanup exists but is scoped to session tables rather than memory_index delete_after retention.
Query/context handlers can selectively self-heal stale graph slices on invocation, but full scan remediation is manual.
Skill graph scan can run via startup/watcher/daemon paths; validation is an explicit manual diagnostic.
Drift explanation dispatches only on explicit tool request and requires a specific memoryId.
Learning history is a read-only query over session_learning rows; no automated feedback loop consumer was found.
Advisor status detects stale/trust state but does not invoke the separate rebuild helper.
The context handler self-checks readiness only after manual dispatch; routing paths recommend rather than invoke it.
Memory health is recommendation-driven and repairs only with explicit confirmation; no diagnostic auto-run was found.
